WB.name: West Bengal
WB.details: Polling for all phases successfully concluded on April 29. West Bengal recorded a massive 92.6% turnout in Phase 2, with an overall turnout of 92.9%. All EVMs are now sealed and stored in robust triple-layered security strongrooms.
WB.voter_demographics: High rural participation, especially in districts like Birbhum and East Midnapore.

TN.name: Tamil Nadu
TN.details: Polling concluded with a historic 85.15% turnout, the highest in the state's election history. EVMs are secured in strongrooms with 24/7 CCTV and paramilitary guard. Counting begins at 8 AM on May 4.
TN.high_turnout_notable: 84% observed in specific rural pockets of Dharmapuri and Karur.

Kerala.name: Kerala
Kerala.details: Polling safely concluded with a final turnout of 73.9%. Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) are sealed and stored in triple-layered security zones.

Assam.name: Assam
Assam.details: Polling concluded with a final turnout of 84%. VVPAT slips will be verified as per EC random sampling protocols on counting day.

Puducherry.name: Puducherry
Puducherry.details: Democratic exercise completed. Results will be declared on May 4.

key_candidates: Samples from various parties including AINRC, DMK, BJP, AIADMK

Tollygunge.details: Polling successfully concluded along with earlier phases on April 29.

Thiruvananthapuram.notable: High urban voter awareness, historically high turnout.

Dispur.details: Capital region, high security during polls.

mla_vs_mp.MLA: Member of Legislative Assembly. Elected for State Governments. They work on state-specific issues like agriculture, state health, and police.
mla_vs_mp.MP: Member of Parliament. Elected for the National Government (Lok Sabha). They work on national issues like defense, railways, and foreign policy.
mla_vs_mp.summary: MLA = State Level, MP = National Level. In 2026, elections are for MLAs.

home_voting.eligibility: Senior citizens (85+ years) and Persons with Disabilities (PwD) with 40% benchmark disability.
home_voting.process: Apply via Form 12D with the Booth Level Officer (BLO) within 5 days of election notification.
home_voting.execution: Two polling officials visit the voter's home with a postal ballot, ensuring complete secrecy.
home_voting.safety: The process is videographed and accompanied by security personnel and party representatives.

general_facts.evm_vvpat: EVM is the electronic device used to record votes. VVPAT (Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail) is a connected machine that prints a paper slip showing your choice for 7 seconds before it drops into a sealed box. It allows you to verify that your vote went to the candidate you selected.
general_facts.nota_info: NOTA (None of the Above) is the last option on the EVM. It allows voters to register a 'no confidence' vote against all candidates in the constituency if they find none suitable.
general_facts.constitutional_rights: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution vests the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission of India.

edge_cases.tendered_votes: Under Section 49P, if a voter finds that someone else has already voted in their name, they can still cast a 'Tendered Vote' using a ballot paper after verifying their identity.
edge_cases.challenge_votes: Under Section 49E, a polling agent can challenge a voter's identity by depositing a small fee. The Presiding Officer then conducts an immediate inquiry.

general_facts.neutrality_policy: As a Digital Assistant of the Election Commission, I provide facts, schedules, and process information. I do not provide political predictions or opinions on who might win.

pro_tips.postal_ballots: Mainly for service voters and those on election duty. Senior citizens (85+) can now also opt for this via Form 12D.
pro_tips.booth_app: The 'Voter Helpline App' can be used to find your exact booth, serial number in the roll, and and e-EPIC download.
pro_tips.security: Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) are deployed at 'Critical' booths to ensure free and fair polling.
pro_tips.counting_agents: Each candidate can appoint counting agents to monitor the transparency of the counting process on May 4.

general_facts.election_commission: Established in 1950, ECI is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.
